transcript.text: This is what I think about the miracles of Jesus.
I kind of just thought of this actually over the last couple of days
Because I saw a movie about Jesus.
A couple movies about Jesus. I saw the movie Jesus and I saw
The Greatest Story Ever Told. They're both really good.
And a whole lot of part about the story of Jesus is the miracles.
And there's lots of different people who claim they saw the miracles.
You know, all four of his apostles that wrote those gospels.
And the miracles happened in front of lots of people.
He brought a dead guy back to life. He created, he turned water to wine.
He created bread for hundreds of people out of thin air.
He resurrected. He made a crippled guy walk, a blind guy see.
Now, the question is, was all this stuff made up by people to try to get people to believe in his teachings and their religion?
Or, did the miracles really happen?
Well, I have entertained both thoughts.
I have, you know, thought, oh, well, you know, he didn't, there were no miracles and he was just a guy who taught.
And then I was thinking, maybe not, maybe it's miracles did happen.
And I'm thinking now that the miracles actually did happen.
And the reason I believe that is because every religion believes in miracles and has tales of miracles.
The Buddhists have tales of miracles.
And I've actually seen miracles myself in my own life.
I've mentioned this a million times before on my videos, but a crack in my windshield that went all the way across completely disappeared one day.
And my teeth completely came together three days after that happened.
And then lots of people talk about, you know, how the hand of, you know, Jesus and Mary suddenly will bleed and churches and stuff like this.
And if it happens, if so many people talk about it, then how would it not?
How could it be a lie?
And then all of the, and the life itself is a miracle.
You know, just looking at my garden and watching just grass and weeds bubble up, you know, and every couple of months you have to fill up all these bags of leaves.
And then, you know, that seems like the dirt's just growing right out of the, of the, of the earth there.
Um, like sunlight creates, just creates stuff and just stuff happens.
And, you know, and then all the aliens and the, what the aliens say and that we're gonna, and the whole concept of heaven, everything and all this stuff happening here.
Now, but also, um, I think it would be possible.
And, you know, if I see the miracles and why wouldn't, why wouldn't Jesus be able to do it?
And maybe Jesus, so then who was this guy?
Who was Jesus?
He must not have been a real human.
Uh, maybe he was an alien.
Now they even say that he wasn't even born of, uh, you know, I'm thinking at this point right now that he was born.
He was an alien because, um, those three wise men knew about his birth before he was born and they came and visited his parents the night he was born.
Um, you know, it was, his birth was prophesied.
He wasn't even conceived by his father, but it was by his mother.
So maybe, then maybe genetically he was an alien and he was conceived by an alien.
And then he, so he had special alien DNA that could, that could, uh, understand that kind of stuff better.
Um, now, do I always believe that?
No, sometimes I go, oh, well, yeah, that could have been not true.
I mean, 2000 years ago, there's all, all people can make up all kinds of stuff and then, you know, and then it wouldn't, uh, that couldn't turn out to not be true.
But the thing is, is so many people witnesses, those, those, those miracles that, um, it must have happened.
Now, another reason why I do believe in the miracles is because the kind of stuff that he was teaching at that time, turn the other cheek.
Um, if someone steals your coat, give them your, your other coat.
Um, and those days, yeah.
And then, and then also, you know, just believe in, in God for everybody.
In those days, that stuff was seen as weakness and it was revolutionary thinking, turn the other cheek and give them the other clothes.
I mean, that was like a dog eat dog world back then.
That was seen as weakness.
Now, somebody going around preaching that, uh, some, you know, regular guy, I don't think would catch on unless he had some, um, some helper things happening like the miracles.
Now, if somebody would say that stuff and people would be like, oh, you're crazy.
And I'm sure, no, I don't, I don't want to like disrespect people in the ancient times.
There's lots of really smart people in the ancient times.
And, you know, I'm just as smart as we are now genetically, just as smart, the same people, but, um, but to come and, and I've done those concepts.
I'm sure I've also been around for a long time before that, but, but, um, for it to catch on like that.
Um, I think, uh, it would have needed all those miracles because people, let's face it, people were really primitive back then.
We had slavery.

We had, uh, I mean, the Roman civilization, you know, we, it wasn't like a civilization like today, you know, you had people, you had the top politicians killing each other, conniving, you know, it was like mafia.
Mafia, oh, so everywhere.
So somebody teaching that kind of hippie stuff that Jesus was teaching.
Um, I think he would have had to have some help, like from the miracles.
Um, now if I was, uh, like a overseer and I think this, our planet does have overseers, caretakers, keepers, aliens.
If I was that to a planet, I would, that's what I would do.
Um, I'd bring a person down to teach that kind of stuff and then I would throw in a bunch of miracles to, to get people's attention.
And, um, and technologically you could do that kind of stuff.
You could hypnotize, you could, you know, hypnotize.
People get abducted by aliens and then they lose time and then they, they show up and it's three hours later, but they don't know.
I mean, people didn't have watches back then.
Maybe, maybe they just hypnotized all the people at the, or abducted all the people at that wedding, switched out the water and the wine and then started it up again.
I don't know.
There's all kinds of ways I'm sure you can do that.
Um, you know, and the, the bread, maybe somebody, some alien, some flying saucer that was invisible came and dropped a whole bunch of bread.
I don't know.
So, but, uh, thing is, is, yeah, I think I do.
I think I do believe in the miracles that they really did happen.
Um, because, uh, miracles happen today.
So why not back then?
Um, yeah.
So that's what I think.
Yes.
Yes.
